post.description: Creates a new user account.
Authenticates a user and returns access + refresh tokens.
Refreshes access token using a valid refresh token
Invalidates a single refresh token, effectively logging out from one device.
Revokes all refresh tokens for the authenticated user.
Initiates password reset by sending a reset link to the user's email.
Completes password reset using the token sent via email.
Verifies the user's email using the confirmation token.
Resends the email verification link (if account exists and email not confirmed).

get.description: Validates the current JWT token and returns session information including email confirmation status.

post.description: Creates test users, a test space, pending invites/join requests, and invite codes.

get.description: Dumps a quick summary of essential database entities for testing.

post.description: Add a new member to the space directly bypassing all invitations/join requests
Wipes all tables in the database and re-applies all migrations.

get.description: Generates and returns a valid authorization bearer token for any username without needing password validation.

post.description: Manually triggers all database housekeeper cleanup jobs (invite codes, direct invites, etc.) using a custom retention threshold.

get.description: Returns all notifications for the current user, ordered by newest first. Supports cursor-based pagination via 'before' query parameter. Notifications are triggered by: space invites, mentions in chat, join request responses, and system announcements. Used to populate the notification bell/panel in the UI.

put.description: Marks a single notification as read. Used when the user clicks on or views a specific notification.
Marks all unread notifications as read for the current user. Used for the 'Mark all as read' button in the notification panel.

get.description: Returns the number of unread notifications for the current user. Used to display the badge count on the notification bell icon. This is a lightweight endpoint designed for frequent polling.

get.summary: Get and search public user profiles
get.description: Returns a paginated list of public user profiles. Use query parameter 'q' to search by username or display name.
Returns the profile details of the currently authenticated user

patch.description: Updates the profile of the currently authenticated user. Only fields present in the request body are updated. Omitted (null) fields remain unchanged. To clear an optional field (Bio, AvatarColor), send an empty string.

delete.description: Permanently deletes the currently authenticated user's account. Requires password confirmation. User must transfer ownership of any owned spaces first.

patch.description: Uploads a new avatar image for the currently authenticated user. Accepts multipart/form-data with a single image file (jpg, jpeg, png, webp). Max size: 2 MB.

file.description: Image file (jpg, jpeg, png, webp). Max 2 MB.

delete.description: Removes the avatar image of the currently authenticated user and generates a new random avatar color

get.summary: Get the user's profile by id
get.description: Returns the public profile of a user by their ID

patch.description: Updates the privacy settings of the currently authenticated user. Supports partial updates.
Initiates an email change. Requires password confirmation. A confirmation link is sent to the new email address.

post.description: Confirms the email change using the token sent to the new email address. This endpoint is called from the confirmation link.

get.description: Unified search across the user's entire workspace — spaces, members, chat channels, and files. Designed for the Ctrl+K command palette experience. Returns categorized results ranked by relevance. Optional 'type' filter narrows results to a specific category (space, member, channel, file). Results only include entities the current user has access to.

post.description: Creates a new space for the currently logged in user

get.description: Get all spaces that the current user is part of (paginated)
Returns a paginated list of public spaces. Use optional query parameter 'q' to search by name or description.
Get space details by ID

delete.description: Delete a space by its ID

patch.description: Update space basic info like name & description
Update space privacy settings (eg. Private, Public)

post.description: Toggle favorite space by its ID (favorite/unfavorite)

get.description: Get all spaces that the current user is part of and favorites it (paginated)

patch.description: Updates the space thumbnail color and/or image file. Used in space settings to customize the space's appearance in the sidebar and cards. Requires ManageSpace permission.

get.description: Returns all banned users from this space (paginated) - requires permission

post.description: Ban a member from this space - requires permission

delete.description: Unban a user for this space - requires permission

get.description: Returns all text chat channels in this space (paginated). Every space has a default 'general' channel created automatically. Used to populate the channel sidebar in the space view.

post.description: Creates a new text chat channel in this space. Channels organize conversations by topic (e.g., #announcements, #team-a, #random). Requires admin or owner permissions.

put.description: Updates the name and/or description of a text channel. Used in channel settings. Requires admin or owner permissions.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a text channel and all its messages. The default 'general' channel cannot be deleted. Requires owner permissions.

get.description: Returns paginated messages for a specific channel, newest first. Supports cursor-based pagination via 'before' query parameter. Used to load chat history when a user opens a channel or scrolls up. Real-time messages arrive via SignalR; this is for historical data.

post.description:
Sends a text message to a channel via REST API. Supports thread replies via the optional ParentId field. In production, prefer SignalR for real-time messaging.

Accepts either multipart/form-data or application/json.

**multipart/form-data fields** (use this when attaching files):
- `Text` (string, required): The message text.
- `ParentId` (guid, optional): ID of the parent message for thread replies.
- `AttachmentFileIds` (guid[], optional): IDs of previously uploaded files to attach.
- `AttachmentUrls` (string[], optional): External URLs to attach.
- `Files` (file[], optional): File uploads to attach to the message.

**application/json body** (use this for text-only messages):
```json
{
  "text": "Hello world",
  "parentId": null,
  "attachmentFileIds": [],
  "attachmentUrls": []
}
```

put.description:
Edits an existing text message. Users can only edit their own messages.

Accepts either multipart/form-data or application/json.

**multipart/form-data fields** (use this when attaching files):
- `Text` (string, required): The updated message text.
- `AttachmentFileIds` (guid[], optional): IDs of previously uploaded files to attach.
- `AttachmentUrls` (string[], optional): External URLs to attach.
- `Files` (file[], optional): File uploads to attach to the message.

**application/json body** (use this for text-only edits):
```json
{
  "text": "Updated message",
  "attachmentFileIds": [],
  "attachmentUrls": []
}
```

delete.description: Deletes a text message. Users can delete their own messages, and users with ManageMessages permission can delete others' messages.

post.description: Forwards an existing message to another target channel within the same space. Copies text and attachments to the new target channel.

get.description: Returns all top-level folders and files at the root of this space's storage. This is the initial view when a user navigates to the Files tab. Items can be folders, uploaded files, or shareable links.
Returns the contents of a specific folder — nested folders and files. Used for folder navigation in the file browser.

delete.description: Deletes a folder and all its contents (files, links, subfolders). This action is irreversible. Requires appropriate permissions.

post.description: Creates a new folder in this space's storage. Optionally nest inside another folder via the ParentId field. Used to organize uploaded files and links into a directory structure.

Uploads a file to this space's storage. Accepts multipart/form-data with a file field and optional folderId. Uploaded files are validated for type safety (magic bytes + text heuristic). Files appear as interactive objects in the 3D world (PDF book, projector slide, etc.).
Creates a link bookmark in this space's storage. Links can reference external resources (Google Docs, Figma, YouTube, etc.) and appear as clickable items in the file browser.

delete.description: Deletes a single file or link item from storage. For files, also removes the stored binary from cloud storage. This action is irreversible.

put.description: Renames an existing folder. Sibling conflict validation is enforced.
Renames a file or link item. Sibling conflict validation is enforced.
Moves a folder to another parent folder. Circular dependency validation is enforced.
Moves an item to another parent folder.

post.description: Recursively copies a folder and all its contents to a target folder.
Copies an item (duplicating physical storage files if applicable) to a target folder.
Allows a user to join a space by providing a valid invite code. This is the endpoint called when a user clicks a shared invite link.

get.summary: List direct invites for the current user
get.description: Returns all pending direct space invites received by the authenticated user.

get.summary: List join requests made by the current user
get.description: Returns all space join requests submitted by the authenticated user.

put.summary: Accept or reject a direct invite
put.description: Allows the invited user to accept or reject a direct invitation. On accept, the user is automatically added as a member of the space.

post.description: Generates a shareable invite code for this space. Used when a space admin wants to create a link they can share on Discord, WhatsApp, etc. Optionally set max uses and expiration date.

get.description: Returns all active (non-expired, non-exhausted) invite codes for this space (paginated). Used in the space settings panel to manage existing invite links.

delete.description: Permanently deactivates an invite code so it can no longer be used. Used when an admin wants to stop a previously shared link from being used.

post.description: Sends direct invitations to multiple users by their usernames or Emails. The target users receive a notification and can accept or reject. Used when an admin wants to invite multiple people at once.

get.description: Returns all pending direct invites sent from this space (paginated). Used in the space settings to see who has been invited and their response status.

delete.description: Allows an admin or someone with right permissions to revoke a direct invitation. Used when an admin wants to cancel an invitation sent to a user before they respond.

get.description: Returns all pending join requests for this space (paginated). Used in the admin panel to review and approve/reject users who want to join.

post.description: Submits a join request to this space. Only applicable for private spaces that have join-request enabled. The request is reviewed by a space admin. An optional message can be included to explain why the user wants to join.

put.summary: Accept or reject a join request
put.description: Allows a space admin to accept or reject a user's join request. On accept, the user is automatically added as a space member.

get.description: Get all space members (paginated)

delete.description: Removes a member from the space. The kicked user can rejoin via a new invite. Cannot kick the owner. Requires KickMembers permission.

put.description: Changes a member's base SpaceRole (Admin, Moderator, Member, Guest). This determines their default permission tier. Cannot change the owner's role — use transfer ownership instead. Requires ManageRoles permission.

post.summary: Assign a custom role to member
post.description: Assigns a custom role to a member. Members can have multiple custom roles — their effective permissions are the union of their base SpaceRole defaults plus all assigned custom roles. Requires ManageRoles permission.

delete.summary: Remove a custom role from member
delete.description: Removes a custom role from a member. The member loses the permissions granted by that role. Requires ManageRoles permission.

get.description: Returns all roles defined for this space, including built-in roles (Owner, Admin, Member) and any custom roles. Used in the space settings and when assigning roles to members.

post.description: Creates a new custom role with specific permissions for this space. Permissions are passed as an array of permission name strings (e.g., ["BanMembers", "ManageChannels"]). Use GET /api/spaces/permissions to see all available permission names. Used by space owners/admins to set up granular access control.

put.description: Updates the name, permissions, and/or color of an existing custom role. Built-in roles (Owner, Admin, Member) cannot be modified. Used in space settings to adjust access control.

delete.description: Deletes a custom role from this space. Members with this role will lose its permissions. Built-in roles cannot be deleted.

get.description: Returns the full list of available permissions with their key, label, description, and category. Used to render the role permission editor UI (checkbox grid grouped by category). This is a reference endpoint — it does not depend on any specific space.

Returns all sessions (active and past) for this space (paginated). Sessions represent live collaborative room instances where users connect via WebSocket for real-time interaction (avatar movement, whiteboard, voice). Used to show session history and find active sessions to join.

post.description: Creates a new collaborative session in this space. The session gets a unique join code that other users can enter to join. Once created, the host can start the session. Max participants controls room capacity. Used when a user clicks 'Start Room' or 'New Session' in the 3D space.

get.description: Returns details of a specific session including its join code, status, and participant count. Used when a user clicks on a session to view its details or prepare to join. The join code is what users enter in the Unity/Flutter client to connect.

put.description: Ends an active session, disconnecting all participants. Only the session host or space owner/admin can end a session. After ending, the session record is preserved for history.

Bearer.description:
Enter 'Bearer' [space] and then your valid JWT token.

Example: "Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9..."
